Optional provenance/attribution for externally-sourced assets (PRD 0002

as Unsplash; export plugins read it to emit the required photographer +
source credit. NOTE: the in-memory registry's freeze reconstructor is
extended to preserve this field in Phase 1 (M5); the type is additive here.

Maximum **raw file size** in bytes (default 1 MB). This bounds the input
file, NOT the emitted

URL: base64 encoding inflates the payload
~4/3, so a file at the cap becomes a ~1.33× larger string that is held in
memory and embedded inline in the registry / IR / exported page (C3). Size
the cap for the *encoded* footprint your target can carry.

content.text:
directives a host page needs in order for the
configured adapters and their served URLs to load. Pure static
derivation; no network or DOM dependencies.

The host calls this once at boot with a description of which
adapters are wired in, then merges the result into its existing CSP
builder.

DEV ONLY — ships the key to the browser; ignored when
; the package never bundles a key.
Throttle floor between JSON requests. Default 1200.
Opt-in re-host instead of hotlinking. Default false.

Per-request timeout for search / topic / photo lookups (ms). A request
that exceeds it aborts and surfaces as a retryable
error, so a blocked or flaky network path (e.g. a VPN/proxy black-holing

) lets the sidebar fall back to "Unsplash unavailable"
instead of spinning. Default 15_000. Ignored if not a positive number.

summary.text: Dev/demo upload adapter that inlines the file as a

URL. The result is
self-contained (no host backend) but ~33% larger than the raw bytes once
base64-encoded — see
Library-management kind inference. Used by both
(sidebar projection) so a single source of truth drives both.

. Preserved as the documented
Studio-side projection so existing imports keep working; library
search now uses the same logic via
In-memory upload adapter for demos and tests. Returns a

URL
that the consumer renders directly, so the object URL intentionally
lives for the page lifetime — it cannot be revoked while the asset is
still referenced.

Lifecycle caveat (C4): this is a stateless factory with no delete hook, so a

-d — including when its asset is
removed from the registry. Each upload pins its

in memory until the
page unloads, so a long session that churns through many upload/delete cycles
leaks. Acceptable for its dev/test purpose; use a real backend (e.g.

Pixel height of a single row when virtualizing.

**Fixed-height contract:** the windowing math assumes every row is
roughly

tall. Rows that wrap or vary in height (long
names, thumbnails) will desync the scroll-into-view calculation. Keep
rows uniform, or raise

so the list renders
inline instead.

Adapters should throw

for HTTP 5xx, network
failures, and other recoverable conditions; non-retryable errors
(4xx, schema mismatches, abort) should be thrown as plain

overrides the next computed delay,
useful when the server returned a

Base delay in milliseconds. The actual delay grows exponentially
(
Cap on the computed backoff delay (before jitter).
Maximum number of retry attempts after the initial call.
Aborts both the in-flight call and any pending retry sleep.

between attempts and during sleep.

Resolution order on each error:
 1. If the signal is aborted → throw

immediately.
 2. If the error is not a

→ rethrow.
 3. If retries are exhausted → rethrow the last error.
 4. Otherwise compute backoff, sleep, and try again.
